meaning: adv.一般地,流行地,通俗地,大众地,
examples: Walesa was Poland's first popularly elected president.
Schizophrenia is not a "split mind" as is popularly believed.
The driving force behind much of this change was an innovation popularly known as the Internet.
Envy and hatred of the rich is the last popularly accepted prejudice in America.
The lower house is popularly elected, and he has yet to call new elections.
